vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/lost. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as the registered species; the pots contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P3: The single Pothos leaf remains stable but shows signs of chlorosis or nutrient stress. The rabbit anchor is stationary.
plant_audit.P1_P2_P4: These pots are currently devoid of viable plant matter, containing only soil substrate and scattered debris (eggshells/perlite).

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently damp across all pots, with no signs of crusting or desiccation.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells, likely an intentional soil amendment or pest deterrent.
biome_observations.surface: Desk surface is clean; no signs of fungal bloom or external contamination.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single leaf in P3 and a small sprout in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: The foreground sprout shows signs of wilting and eventual collapse/disappearance.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The biome has reached a state of stasis with only the single Pothos leaf remaining.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. Only one specimen (P3) remains viable. The lack of growth in other pots suggests either failed propagation or total mortality of the original specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshells is a notable intervention. The 'foreground pot' (likely P4 or P2) has lost its primary sprout, confirming a systemic failure of the intended botanical occupants.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a struggling indoor biome. While the Pothos leaf in P3 maintains a baseline level of health, the surrounding pots have suffered total loss of their intended occupants. The environment is stable but unproductive, with the eggshell debris suggesting an attempt at soil enrichment that has not yet yielded new growth.
